beats.activity: Woke up, made a quiet non-drip breakfast, and did a short checklist for the day while the drizzle started outside.
beats.reasoning: Rain in the morning favors a slow routine at home, and Lucas avoids drip coffee.

beats.activity: Packed his bag, grabbed an umbrella, and reviewed a couple student notes before leaving.
beats.reasoning: A quick stop for resources/forms fits his counselor routine while the rain is steady.

beats.activity: Drove toward Mercer Island with extra buffer for wet roads and expected SR-520 congestion.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closure 9am–noon increases uncertainty, so he departs early and takes a slower route.

beats.activity: Counseling day: check-ins, behavior plan follow-ups, and supporting a few students with coordinated steps for class.
beats.reasoning: Core job responsibilities fill the rainy midday hours indoors.

beats.activity: Grabbed a warm, quick dinner to go and kept moving so he wouldn’t get stuck in evening traffic.
beats.reasoning: Drizzle continues until 2pm; after school he prefers quick indoor options while staying efficient.

beats.activity: Returned across the water toward Woodinville, choosing a route that avoids the worst of the 520 closure ripple.
beats.reasoning: The SR-520 eastbound closure (9am–noon) leaves downstream congestion; he budgets extra time.

beats.activity: Did a small grocery run for brewing ingredients and easy rainy-day cooking staples.
beats.reasoning: He’s community-minded and likes reliable quality ingredients; rain makes errands short and indoor.

beats.activity: Brewing prep at home: measured grains, sanitized equipment, and started a batch.
beats.reasoning: Home brewing is his decompress ritual, and staying in matches his introverted, weather-aware mood.

beats.activity: Had a quiet snack, then did a short indoor stretch and checked tomorrow’s counseling schedule.
beats.reasoning: He prefers low-key recovery after a full workday, especially with drizzle limiting outdoor plans.

beats.activity: Texted two close friends and met one of them briefly for a calm, non-crowded catch-up at a nearby cozy spot.
beats.reasoning: He values time with close friends over big crowds; light social time fits his introversion.

beats.activity: Picked up a few last supplies for the brew (yeast and hop storage) and headed back before it gets later.
beats.reasoning: Even with rain, he runs and brews; REI is a reliable place for related gear without a big crowd.

beats.activity: Wind-down at home: cleaned up the brewing area, listened to calm music, and wrote a few notes for students.
beats.reasoning: He stays productive but relaxed at night, and the next beat is home wind-down before sleep.

beats.activity: Went to sleep after a quiet evening, letting the day’s stress fade with the brew settling in the background.
beats.reasoning: He ends the day asleep at home, and sleep spans overnight ending by 6:00.

diary: Grey Tuesday again—drizzle hanging in the air like it forgot how to leave. I started at home in Woodinville with a quiet, non-drip breakfast and a little checklist, mostly just to keep my head clear before the Mercer Island school day. By the time I rolled up to the Woodinville Library, I’d tucked my umbrella in like it was part of my job. The rain made everything feel slower, and SR-520 being closed eastbound from 9 to noon only amplified it—my commute felt like negotiating with water and headlights. At Mercer Island High School, counseling was steady: check-ins, behavior plan follow-ups, and those brief moments where you can actually see a student exhale. After a warm go-to dinner from Pike Place Market, I detoured around the worst of the traffic and got back for brewing. Measuring grains at home felt oddly grounding. Even with the wet roads, I ended the night calmer than I started—like the day cooled off with the beer settling.
thought_process: I planned for the weather and the SR-520 closure like it was another student schedule—buffers, backups, and no “I’ll just wing it” moments. Drizzly roads always make me overestimate time, so I left with extra slack and kept my counseling priorities tight once I got to Mercer Island High School. During work, I focused on follow-ups and coordinated steps because those are the things that prevent small issues from turning into bigger ones. The bridge closure did affect my mood—there was a brief irritation during the morning crawl—but it faded once I was in the counselor rhythm, steady and human. Lunch and the Pike Place Market detour were about staying warm and not getting stuck later. Brewing at home was my reset button; by evening, my thoughts felt quieter and more hopeful.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
